title: Cauldron Dyeing
description: Bring Bedrock's cauldron-based armor dyeing to Java Edition.
body:
# Cauldron Dyeing This mod adds Bedrock's ability to dye leather armor or leather horse armor with cauldrons to Java Edition.
![dyed water](https://static.violet.sh/images/dyed-water.jpg)
The usage is the same as in Bedrock, using dye on a cauldron with water will color the water, adding dye multiple times will blend the dyes same as in a crafting table. When you use a dyeable item on the cauldron, it will add the current color in the cauldron to the item. Using a water bucket on a cauldron with dyed water will reset the color.
*Using a dyed item on an un-dyed water cauldron will still remove its color.*
## Installation Make sure you have the latest version of [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/) or [Quilt Loader](https://quiltmc.org/install/) installed. To install, just drag the mod jar into your mods folder along with either [FAPI](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api) or [QFAPI](https://modrinth.com/mod/qsl).
## Compatibility Cauldron Dyeing is ensured to be compatible with (Quilted) Fabric API, CaffeineMC mods, and Iris, if any incompatibility is found please report it on the [issue tracker](https://github.com/TibiNonEst/cauldron-dyeing/issues).
**Note:** The current method of displaying dyed water is not compatible with shaders that customize water color such as Complementary's Realistic-ish and RTX-ish settings.
## Issues Report any bugs, crashes, or other issues that may arise on the [issue tracker](https://github.com/TibiNonEst/cauldron-dyeing/issues).
